stage: v0.3 P2.9 Benchmark — KB-5 Gap Closure
decision: Use MCP search tools (mcp_paper-search + mcp_english-search) to close the KB-5 domain knowledge gap. KB-5 (AfterValidator annotation propagation through FastAPI dependency analysis) was the only remaining false negative across 3 benchmark projects. The root cause was identified via MCP search: FastAPI PR #13442 documented that PR #13314's Pydantic 2.11 compat change inadvertently stripped Annotated metadata (AfterValidator, BeforeValidator) from the dependency analysis chain. This knowledge was synthesized into a reusable reference file (references/python-pydantic-audit-rules.md) with 3 detection rules, following the security-best-practices skill's references/ directory pattern. The reference file can be loaded by v0.5 P3.1 guards.yml system.
rationale: KB-5 was the sole remaining false negative (1/13 unknown bugs missed, 92.3% detection rate). MCP search tools provided access to the actual GitHub PRs and Pydantic API documentation that generic web search could not surface with sufficient depth. The search confirmed: (1) PR #13314 broke Annotated metadata propagation, (2) PR #13442 reverted + added tests, (3) the detection pattern involves checking typing.get_args(annotated_type) processing in dependencies/utils.py and _compat.py. Writing this as a reference file rather than hardcoding into SKILL.md keeps the architecture pluggable (v0.5 P3.1 guards.yml pattern). The MCP search pattern — find PR → read docs → synthesize detection rules → persist to reference file — is now validated as a repeatable knowledge-gap-closure workflow.
next_action: Proceed to v0.4: Implement P2.11 Iterative Audit + P2.13 Repair Guidance + P2.10 Deterministic Assist Layer. The KB-5 reference file will be loaded when v0.5 P3.1 guards.yml system is implemented.

decision: Separate knowledge extraction (deterministic Python scripts via RunCommand) from knowledge injection (subagent prompt augmentation). Three extractors: rule_extractor.py (benchmark→guard), spec_graph.py (spec↔code mapping), inter_rater.py (P2.7 kappa). One injector: SKILL.md Step 0.5 Knowledge Loading (RAG pattern).
rationale: Extraction must be deterministic and auditable; injection can be AI-assisted. Separating them enforces the 'human reviews rules, AI checks compliance' principle from ROADMAP §Design Constraints. All extractors are pure Python stdlib (no external deps beyond PyYAML), capped at 30s timeout, fail→skip on error.
next_action: Implement 7-task TDD plan: test_v20 (RED) → rule_extractor → spec_graph → inter_rater → SKILL.md → ROADMAP → regression (118/118 GREEN).

stage: v2.0 Architecture — Code Review Findings (7 deviations)
decision: After thorough codebase audit of all 12 actual files (benchmark JSONs, guards YAMLs, schema JSON, rule_index.py, verify_lines.py, merge_guards.py, score_tracker.py), 7 analysis deviations were found and corrected: (1) audit-log overcount 5→4, (2) P2.7 (Dual-Subagent Voting) was the only remaining undelivered P2 in ROADMAP → incorporated into v2.0, (3) spec_graph effort underestimated → split into v2.0 mapping layer + v2.1 AST integration, (4) benchmark→guard auto-generalization overpromised → v2.0 does structural conversion only, LLM generalization deferred to v2.2, (5) community rule sharing needs user base → independent repo deferred to v2.3, (6) code knowledge graph overlaps with CodeGraph → ADD focuses on spec↔code mapping layer, (7) v2.0 scope too broad → reduced to minimum viable knowledge pipeline validation.
rationale: These corrections prevent overengineering and ensure v2.0 delivers verified value within 13-day scope. The most critical finding — benchmark evidence field format inconsistency across 3 projects — drove a 3-tier fallback parser in rule_extractor.py.
next_action: Execute corrected 7-task TDD plan. Key deliverable: 3 new scripts, 22 TDD checks, 96 regression, all GREEN.

decision: Discovered that benchmark TP evidence field has 3 different formats across projects: Flask has pure text (no file:line), FastAPI retest has mixed formats, Requests retest has clean file:line format. Designed parse_evidence() with structured regex (file.py:L123-L456), simple regex (file.py:L123), and 3-tier fallback (known_bugs[].file → scoped_modules inference → unknown.py placeholder).
rationale: Rule extractor must handle real-world data format inconsistency without failing. The fallback ensures every TP produces a guard entry (≥8 guards required), even if some have approximate file paths. Guards with fallback paths carry confidence: auto-extracted and require human review before moving to guards.project.yml.
next_action: Implement parse_evidence() with all 3 fallback tiers. Test against actual Flask/FastAPI/Requests benchmark JSONs.
